OpenBlue platform combines the Company's building expertise with cutting-edge technology, including artificial intelligence and machine learning-powered service solutions such as remote diagnostics, predictive maintenance, workplace management, compliance monitoring and advanced risk assessments

increased maturity and adoption of AI and high-performance computing is currently impacting the microchip and data center industry...driving technology innovation, which has led to increased demand for hyperscale and data center cooling solutions

introduction of these technologies, particularly generative AI, into internal processes and/or new and existing offerings may result in new or expanded risks and liabilities, including due to enhanced governmental or regulatory scrutiny, litigation, compliance issues, ethical concerns, confidentiality or security risks

use of AI in the development of our products and services could also cause loss or theft of intellectual property, as well as subject us to risks related to intellectual property infringement or misappropriation, data privacy and cybersecurity

AI can lead to unintended consequences, including generating content that appears correct but is factually inaccurate, misleading or otherwise flawed, or that results in unintended biases and discriminatory outcomes, which could harm our stakeholders

limitations: No quantified AI-specific capex or budget figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 based on named platform and named use cases without dollar amounts or headcount figures.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed use cases (OpenBlue, remote diagnostics, predictive maintenance) with operating detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., % efficiency gains, revenue attributable) were found in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure scored 4 as multiple company-specific risk mechanisms are described (IP loss, regulatory scrutiny, generative AI errors, competitive disadvantage), but no quantified risk exposure or named governance/oversight body was identified in the excerpts.
